description_long: This ladder features a sturdy frame with three vertical side rails, two of which are colored a light blue and one a darker brown, supporting a series of evenly spaced horizontal rungs. The rungs are cylindrical and appear to be made of wood, with a natural finish that contrasts with the painted side rails. The structure is open and tall, with the rungs extending across the width between the rails, providing ample space for climbing. The design is simple and utilitarian, lacking any visible safety features such as anti-slip feet or handrails. The overall construction suggests it is intended for use in urban environments, such as for accessing higher shelves or maintenance tasks. The materials and build indicate it is lightweight and portable, suitable for temporary placement rather than permanent installation.
description: A tall, three-rail ladder with cylindrical wooden rungs and two light blue side rails, designed for climbing and utility tasks. The structure is open, lightweight, and lacks additional safety features.
description_view_0: Front view showing three vertical side rails and all horizontal rungs clearly, with the light blue rails most prominent.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the ladder and the alignment of the rungs, with the rails appearing as thin vertical lines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the rungs and the brown rail, with the blue rails visible in the background.
description_view_3: Angled top-side view showing the spacing and cylindrical shape of the rungs, as well as the arrangement of the rails.
